title: Missing IV length validation in sm4EncryptInit
problem: When iv parameter is non-NULL, the code checks IV length in decryptInit but not in encryptInit. This could lead to buffer over-read if an incorrectly sized IV is passed.

title: SM2PublicKey exposes internal key arrays
problem: getX() and getY() methods return the internal byte arrays directly instead of clones, allowing external code to modify the key material.

title: DERCodec copyToFixedBuffer has incorrect truncation logic
problem: When srcLen > fixedLen (32 bytes), the code truncates from the end. However, for properly encoded DER integers, when high bit is 0 and there's a leading 0x00 byte (making 33 bytes), this would truncate the wrong byte (the 0x00 prefix should be removed, not trailing bytes).

title: sm3Update lacks null data array check
problem: The function doesn't check if data is NULL before calling GetByteArrayElements. If an empty byte array is passed, GetByteArrayElements could return NULL, leading to null pointer dereference.

title: SM4Cipher uses deprecated finalize() for cleanup
problem: The finalize() method is deprecated and unreliable. Resources may not be released properly. The class should implement AutoCloseable instead.

title: SM3MessageDigest uses deprecated finalize() for cleanup
problem: The finalize() method is deprecated and unreliable. Resources may not be released properly. The class should extend a cleanup pattern.

title: SM2Signature uses deprecated finalize() for cleanup
problem: The finalize() method is deprecated and unreliable. Session resources may leak.

title: SM2KeyPairGenerator uses deprecated finalize() for cleanup
problem: The finalize() method is deprecated and unreliable. Session resources may leak.

title: hexToBytes doesn't validate input length
problem: The method assumes the hex string has even length. If an odd-length string is passed, it will throw IndexOutOfBoundsException instead of a more descriptive error.

title: Maven forkCount changed from 0 to 1
problem: Changing forkCount from 0 (no fork) to 1 (forked process) may cause issues with stateful providers like JCE providers where provider registration needs to be shared.

title: Loaded flag visibility issue in double-checked locking
problem: The `loaded` flag is checked outside the synchronized block without proper volatile semantics. While volatile is declared, the double-check pattern could still have visibility issues on some JVMs.
